text:
AnOlympicswimming coach is at risk of being sent home from the Games before the opening ceremony after making a bizarre comment which has been deemed 'unacceptable'. Michael Palfrey, one of eight coaches forAustralia, has apologised to officials for the comments which have caused a stir around camp.Speaking to a Seoul-based television channel, Palfrey said that he hopes Korean swimmer Kim Woo-min beats Australia duo Sam Short and Elijah Winington. He previously mentored Kim, the reigning 400 metre freestyle champion who won the event at this year's World Aquatics Championships.While wearing Australia team colours, Palfrey said of Kim: "I really hope he can win, but ultimately I really hope he swims well. Go Korea!"

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

The comments have sparked friction within the Australia camp and could see Palfrey sent home from the Games despite making an apology. Swimming Australia head coach Rohan Taylor described the interview as 'un-Australian' and said he was considering expelling Palfrey from the Olympics."I am extremely disappointed," said Taylor. "For a coach on our team to promote another athlete ahead of our athletes is not acceptable. He either goes home or he stays, that's the options, and it's what is the impact on performance of the team."If he goes home, we have to find other coaches to pick up the load on the athletes and our coaches are really stretched, and is that in the athletes' best interest? That's one consideration."If he stays, then it's about the team dynamic and making sure that the [other] coaches are happy with that and can support that and the athletes themselves can go about their business and not be distracted. He made an error and I told him that I don't appreciate it.

"It was an un-Australian comment. Does that mean that he's unworthy of looking after the three athletes he has coached for the last three years to this point, who rely on him to prepare them for the performance? That is what I've got to weigh up."Taylor also said he wanted Palfrey's fate to be determined as quickly as possible to prevent further disruption to Australia's preparations for the Games. Coaches were reportedly given guidelines in March to end any association with non-Australian swimmers before the Olympics.Australia chef de mission Anna Meares added that Palfrey's comments marked a 'serious error of judgment' and questioned whether he was still involved with Korean swimmers."He is very aware and very sorry for what has occurred," said Meares. "We won't be stepping in over the top of Swimming Australia. We will be working with them and any decision that they make, we will support."The fact that he's commented about the athlete winning against the Australian team, that's the bigger issue."

title: Olympics coach may be sent home after making 'unacceptable' comment about rival | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Archie Griggs
description: Australia coach Michael Palfrey has landed himself in hot water ahead of the Games.
